7.0 SITE CLEARANCE

71 Prior to commencement of work, the Contractor shall obtain a clearance from the Safety
Engineering Department of the Employer and concerned department/ section of the
Employer where the job is to be carried out. The Employer/ Engineer shall not permit the
Contractor to commence the work unless Contractor or his representative produces such
clearance certificate. The Department concerned shall grant such clearance after
ensuring that :

a) The Contractor himself has undergone Safety Induction Training organised by the
Employer’s Safety Engineering Department in the preceding twelve months.

b) The Contractor’s workers, identified for particular work / Department, have
undergone Safety Induction Training in the preceding twelve months.

c) The Contractor has arranged PPE and Safety appliances for himself and for all his
workmen, as required.

d) The Contractor possesses work permit issued by the Employer or Employer's
representative for which the “Work Clearance Form ”(Annexure-1B) shall be filled
in triplicate by the Contractor for record of :

i) Safety
ii) Department/ Section of the Employer granting clearance and
iii) Contractor

e) | The Contractor and his supervisors along with the Contract documents and job
instructions received from the employer, will come to Employer’s Safety
Engineering Department where the Contractor will obtain Safety Rule Book for
Contractor and seek clarifications, if any. The Contractor is not permitted to stop
the job without clearance from Employer’s Safety Engineering Department.

f) The Contractor shall possess handling equipment, tools & tackles of adequate
capacity and tested quality.

72 Contractor shall obtain specific permission in accordance with IPSS:1-11-007-01
“Procedure to Permit to Work” and IPSS:1-11-005-98 “Procedure for working at
height by Contractor’s Workers” from the Engineer of the company or the
companies Safety Department before commencing the following:

a) Working in an operating plant / equipment ;

8.0 SKILL AND COMPETENCE OF WORKMEN

8.1 The Contractor shall be responsible to engage competent and skilled workers.

8.2 Before starting of the day’s job, the Contractor shall ensure that safety briefing has been
done to his workers by himself or his supervisors who have been imparted safety
induction earlier and also maintain records to this effect.

9.0 PHYSICAL FITNESS OF WORKMEN

9.1 The Contractor shall ensure that his employees/ workmen subject themselves to such
medical examination as required under the law or under the company’s rule and keep
a record of the same.

9.2 The Contractor shall not permit any employee/ workmen to enter the work area
under the influence of alcohol.

10.0 DEALING WITH ACCIDENT

10.1 The Contractor shall maintain a safety register, keep records of all minor and major
accidents, serious or averted (near miss) accidents, with or without injuries to human
beings and shall submit a report about the accident to the Engineer promptly, on the form

11.0 MISCELLANEOUS

11.1 Safety Records (Statutory Records)

The Contractor shall keep record of :

i) Test certificates of all lifting tools & tackles, pressure vessels etc.

ii) Periodical inspection report of safety appliances,

iii) Maintaining accident analysis report in proper format in consultation with
Employer’s Engineer.

iv) Safety record as per Factory Rules/ Safety Codes or any
other Statutory provision shall also be maintained. However, necessary help in
this regard can be taken from concerned Safety Organization.

11.2 Gas Lines
To work in dangerous gas lines e.g. Coke Ovens gas line, Blast Furnace gas line, LD gas
line etc. Employer will provide job safety induction to the Contractor’s personnel. For this
the Contractor shall give in writing the list of personnel likely to work in gas line (new/
old) to the Engineer who in turn will arrange the safety induction to the Contractor’s
personnel inside Employer’s Works. The Contractor shall ensure the presence of his
personnel at place of safety induction (normally Central Gas Safety Station, E&E
Department) and follow the steps, General rules & regulations as imparted during the
training.

11.3 Electrician/ Other Electrical Personnel

The Contractor shall engage qualified and competent electricians and _ other

electrical personnel while working on electrical lines (which may be High Tension,

Medium Tension and Low Tension electrical lines) for safe execution of Contract. The

electricians and other electrical personnel must possess requisite certificate issued from

competent authority.

Electrical Equipment

a) The Contractor shall ensure proper earthing of all electrical equipment/ machinery

prior to start of the machine. The Contractor shall also ensure daily examination of
the earthing of equipment/ machinery prior to start of the job in order to avoid
electrical hazards.

b) The Contractor shall ensure use of proper cable (may be welding cable or power

cable or control cable). The joints of the cables shall be perfectly insulated. The

Contractor shall also ensure the use of proper plugs, sockets and other electrical

fittings while executing any Contract. ELCB of appropriate rating shall be used by

the Contractor in the power distribution system for safety of personnel working.

11.4 Scaffolding/ lifting/lowering / dismantling of equipment or fabricated structural
or any other materials.

i) For safe execution of the Contract, Contractor shall make a programme of a piece/
whole job, in sequence of steps involved in execution the required safety
measures to be taken during the execution of the job.

ii) The Contractor shall ensure the use of sufficiently strong scaffolding with
sufficiently strong hand railings during the execution of the job. The Employer will
provide prints of “Standard Scaffolding Drawing” on request by the Contractor.
Scaffold/ platform to be used by more than 2 persons has to be approved by the
Site Engineer and the concerned Safety Organisation, jointly. The Contractor shall
ensure the use of scaffoldings at construction site by his personnel working at
height, fabricated as per standard drawing for scaffolding. If the scaffolding as per
the standard drawing is not found suitable at site, the Contractor shall make
necessary modification to strengthen the same. For any civil engineering job, the
Contractor will provide adequate support before casting or any superstructure
and provide approach, scaffolding etc. in such a manner that is safe, easy for
movement of men, women and materials.

iii) The Contractor shall make his own arrangement for the area lighting/ spot

Page 191 of 214
Tender Enquiry No: HO(Works) / CONTRACTS / ERP / SAP AMC / 2024 / 1046 / 215 Dtd. 20.08.2024


---

Page 364 (pytesseract-ocr):
racasimn
<&} ON OF SERVICE 98, FOR SAP S4/HANA ANNUAL MAINTENANCE
> CONTRACT - NMDC
NMDc
lighting at fabrication yard. The Employer will provide power source at one
point only near to the fabrication yard.

iv) The Employer will provide area lighting at erection site only. The Contractor shall
make his own arrangement for spot lighting. The source of power supply at the
nearest possible point will be shown by the engineer at site.

11.5 House keeping

i) The Contractor shall ensure good housekeeping in his working area, fabricating
yard as well as at erection site. The safe approach to the working place must be
ensured. Place for keeping raw materials, semi-finished materials should be
clearly marked with proper identification. Machine component shall be kept
properly under shed.

ii) The Contractor shall keep the scrap material at one place duly barricated and
shall remove the same regularly from the work place.

iii) The Contractor shall stack properly the fabricated or any other items likely to be
erected very shortly at erection site, in such a way that the materials do not cause
congestion at site or create unsafe condition for free and safe approach.

iv) The Contractor should put up a board (minimum 4’ x 3’) indicating the name of
the Contractor, details of the job, order No., starting date and likely completion
date of the job. On completion of the job, the Contractor shall clear the site (good
materials and scrap). The board so displayed shall be taken out of the site. Any
material left after the job is over will be picked up by the Employer and the
Contractor will be charged back for the same.

v) Barrication
The Contractor shall ensure barrication of the unsafe area, which can lead to
accidents to any unknown person working in that area. In order to remove unsafe
condition and prevent accidents, this unsafe area such as trench/ pit/ foundation,
any type of opening made or left in any floor of the building including staircase
etc. must be barricated.

The hand railings shall be immediately provided in all the floors, stairs etc.
before proceeding for erection/ dismantling.

vi) Removal of muck shall be done within 24 hours from road sides.

11.6 The Contractor shall ensure that compressed air is not used for removing the dust from
one’s clothes. Compressed air shall not be blown against any one as it may injure or
even kill him / her.

11.7 The Contractor shall provide, maintain and arrange for periodical checking of adequate
numbers of fire extinguishers of appropriate type and other facilities (e.g. sand buckets,
asbestos covering etc.) in fire prone areas.

11.8 The Contractor shall provide clearly demarcated access and exits at work site to take
care in case of outbreak of fire or any other eventualities.

11.9 The Contractor shall ensure that smoking or keeping of naked light near gas lines, valves
and any other equipment connected with gas distribution system and handling of all
inflammable material is strictly prohibited.

11.10 The Contractor shall ensure that no one takes rest/ shelter below any dumped slag,
boulder or any other cut pit/ excavation near any stock of materials, electrical
installations and gas lines etc.

11.11. TheContractor shall ensure the availability of first aid box at the working sites.

11.12 Make available all necessary personal safety equipments/ appliances such as _ safety
helmets, safety boots, full body harness, gloves, leather gloves for welders, clear glass,
safety goggles and other relevant PPEs as advised by Safety Department or Departmental
Safety Officer for the use of persons employed at the site of work and maintain in
condition suitable for immediate use and shall take steps to ensure proper use of
equipment by the workers.

11.13. Usage of full body harness and anchoring it at rigid place is to be ensured by the
Contractor or his supervisor who shall be present all the time on working spot.

11.14 Use only steel pipe scaffolding.

11.17. In case of contracts involving deployment of workers at a height exceeding 10 meters
the following clauses will also apply -

A) Persons carrying out such jobs should be selected by the contractors within age group

format prescribed by NMDC.

B) All such persons should be medically examined for fitness and fit certificate may be
issued by Plant Hospital, Nagarnar. Rs.500/- per contract labour will be charged
from the Contractors towards this medical examination by Plant Hospital, Nagarnar.

C) Special Training is to be imparted by Safety Department for such persons.

11.18 (I) Operating Authority will assess the penalty amount having regard to all the
circumstances, in particular the nature and gravity of the violation on the advice of the

Head of Safety Department and will issue a show cause notice specifying therein the

proposed penalty. Considering the cause shown by the contractor, if any, the Operating

Authority shall pass final orders which shall then be binding on the contractor. The

penalty amount shall be recoverable from any bill and/ or SD of the contractor without

any further reference to him.

(ID In case of violations from Category (I) & Category (II) i.e. occasional violation and

minor violation, if repeated for the second time and onwards, the penalty will be

recovered, the penalty will be recovered from the dues of the contractor on the advice
from SED (Safety Engineering Department) officials and the Operating Authority will be
kept informed only.

(I For the violations of Category-III and Category-IV as mentioned above and

detailed in the Annexure, if repeated for the second time and onwards, the penalty will

be recovered by the Finance Department against an advice from SED (As decided in
consultation with the Operating Authority within 3 days from the date of violation).

(IV) In case of violation of Category-V i.e. Fatal cases, SED officials will impose fine of

Rs.3,00,000/- or 20% of the contract value whichever is lower on the contractor without

consulting Operating Authority and direct Finance Deptt. to deduct from the contractor’s

11.19 Contractors should ensure that his workers follow all jobs specific safety precautions as

11.20 a) Persons working on platform above 02 meter from the ground level and persons
working on the ground level on the periphery of the pit which is 02 meter or more
deep must wear ISI safety belt and persons working on the platforms which are 05
meter above the ground level, but less than 10 meters, must undergo safety training
for ‘Working at height’ being imparted by Safety Engineering Department and must
also wear safety belt. A certificate for such safety training for ‘Working at height’ will
be issued to the person by Safety Engineering Department valid for a maximum period
of one year.

b) Persons working on the platform above 10 meter from the ground level and
persons working on the ground level on the periphery of the pit which is 10 meter or
more deep must use ISI Safety Belt and obtain medical / mental fitness certificate
from the Medical Officer of the company/ Civil Surgeon, Nagarnar. On the basis of this
certificate, Safety Engineering Department will impart safety training for working at
height and issue height pass. Height pass issued will be in the name of individual
worker and its validity will be for a maximum period of 01 year only.

c) On expiry, certificates of safety training for working at height as per 4(a) and height
passes as per 4(b) will be renewed after the workers passes the tests and imparted
training once again

12.0 Operational Control Procedures:

Purpose:

To establish and maintain the procedure and to ensure that -

i. Operational controls as applicable to the organizations and its activities

ii, Controls related to purchased goods, equipments and services

iii. Controls related to Contractors

iv. Documented procedures to cover situations where their absence could lead to

deviations from OHS policy
v. Stipulating operating criteria

<&} CTION OF SERVICE OR FOR SAP S4/HANA ANNUAL MAINTENANCE
> CONTRACT - NMDC
NMDc

Procedure:

i. Identify those activities, operation and services that are having hazards and risk
assessed and identified and prioritized significant risks where operational control is
required.

ii, Documented operational control procedures shall be prepared to cover the activities,
operations and services.

iii, Operational Control Procedures shall be made available at the place of work in printed
or electronic form.

iv. Critical equipment, machinery and other sources from point of OH & S shall be
identified and their preventive maintenance schedule shall be laid down in order to
reduce / control or prevent incidents. Machines and equipment shall be operated,
monitored as per the Operational Control Procedures and laid down criteria and
records shall be maintained.

v. Controls related to contractors for adherence to all safety precautions and applicable
guidelines.

13.0 Safety Guidelines by Ministry of Steel;

The Contractor shall adopt the Safety guidelines issued by Ministry of Steel while

working at site, the guidelines are available in the following website;

https://steel.gov.in/safety/safety-guidelines-iron-steel-sector

Annexure-1C (contd.)
Note:
1. When aninjury occurs to aContractor’s employee while inside the factory, the injured
person should be sent to Plant Medical Unit immediately, with this form in triplicate.
2. The Medical Officer on duty at Plant Medical Unit will retain one copy and send one copy
to Safety Engineering Department. The third copy shall be returned to the Contractor
concerned after duly filling up the forms.
In case, the Contractor takes the injured person to his private doctor, he shall do so by
giving a written undertaking to the doctor in Plant Medical Unit and to the Manager, Safety
Engineering Department.
If the private doctor declares the injured person unfit for duty more than 2 days, this
becomes a reportable accident under the Factories Act and the Contractor shall
immediately report this to the Manager, Safety Engineering Department for sending
reports to concerned Government agencies.
The Contractor shall also keep the manager (Safety) inform about :
a) The condition of the injured person ;
b) The period of disability and
c) Any loss of earning capacity certified by the doctor.
The Contractor shall produce the fitness certificate from the attending doctor after the
injured person is declared fit for duty.

1. Scope of Work
1.1 This scope of work in this tender includes
To provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted day to
day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP System at all
locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP landscape implemented in
NMDC Limited including developments to be made as per the requirements of business notified
from time to time.
a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)
b) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM)
c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)
d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)
e) HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT (HCM) / EMPLOYEE SELF SERVICE (ESS) /
MANAGEMENT SELF SERVICE (MSS)
f) PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)
g) SALES & DISTRIBUTION (SD)
h) QUALITY MANAGEMENT (QM)
i) ENVIRONMENT, HEALTH & SAFETY (EHS)
j) ADVANCED BUSINESS APPLICATION PROGRAMMING (ABAP)
k) BUSINESS APPLICATION SYSTEMS INTEGRATED SOLUTION (BASIS)
1) MANAGEMENT DASHBOARDS, FIORI APPS
1.2 Support shall be extended from Head Office, Hyderabad to all locations. The major locations
of NMDC are mentioned hereunder for information:
+ BIOM, Kirandul Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)
+ BIOM, Bacheli Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)
* DIOM, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)
* Pellet Plant, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)
+ DMP, Panna. (Madhya Pradesh)
* SIU, Paloncha (Andhra Pradesh)
* Slurry Pipe Line (SPL), Jagdalpur, (Chhattisgarh)
+ R&D Centre, Hyderabad (Telangana)
* Regional Offices Delhi, Raipur, Visakhapatnam, Chennai, Bengaluru, Mumbai, Bhubaneshwar,
Kolkata.
However, wherever required, the support team has to travel to respective locations as per the
instructions of ERP GM(Mining)-ERP. The Conveyance, Lodging & Boarding charges will be borne by
NMDC as per the terms mentioned in this tender.

1.3 The major locations of NMDC are mentioned SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:

i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support services which

(Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting, Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. The support team should be based full time at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad to facilitate
timely resolution of issues.

v. Enhancements, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as applicable) are to be installed
time to time where ever required on release by OEM in consultation with the company
including third party solutions implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be
accompanied by a detailed document containing the step by step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) in the
system to streamline and have better control across the transactions of the company.

vii. The successful bidder shall provide contact numbers, email addresses, escalation matrix of a
dedicated support team. NMDC shall be kept informed well in advance in case any changes are
being made in the contact details.

Configuration documents, Functional document if any) for each resolved issue GM(Mining)-
ERP, NMDC Limited.

ix. The Dashboards developed and configured through Qlik Sense Software. The Scope of support
comprises maintenance of existing Dashboards including addition / deletion / modification of
specific views as per the business requirements.

x. For changes pertaining to completely new processes, or any new developments including
Dashboards etc., the total efforts (man days) required for the change requests will be mutually
agreed by both the successful bidder and NMDC. If the total efforts required are not more than
5-man days, then the successful bidder will implement the change free of cost. In case of
changes requiring efforts more than 5-man days, NMDC LIMITED will obtain a management
approval for the financial implication to carry out the changes. The financial implication will
be calculated by reducing the agreed effort by 5-man days and then multiplying the balance

Background Tasks, Printer and spool administration / maintenance, Administration and
maintenance of Transport Management System, System Monitoring and Trouble Shooting,
Setting Up an SAP System Landscape, SLD monitoring, Exporting/importing Transport
Requests, , System Workload Analysis, System trace monitoring, Performance Analysis, SAP
Memory Management , System Load Verification, Expensive SQL Statements Analysis, SAP
Table buffering, Database Overview and monitoring, DB Monitoring Tools, Recovery, DBA
activity through BR* Tools, RESTORING a BACKUP without any additional cost to NMDC
LIMITED.

xix. The successful bidder will perform SAP BASIS activities in Development, Quality and
production SAP S/4 HANA systems. They will also perform all the System Monitoring and
health check activities for smooth running of the servers.

parameters of the SAP ERP System at the time of handover from the Existing Contractor.

1.4 SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support Services including
enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC mentioned hereunder. It may note
that the scope mentioned hereunder is not exhaustive:

Role of SAP Functional Consultant

e Assist business users for the problems/issues encountered during day-to-day business
transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

e Study and evaluate NMDC’s users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team Members
& User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an abstract and algorithmic
business model or System Requirement Specifications.

e Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or alternative solution
is not available, solution may be provided through configuration and customizing the respective
business area and ensure to map the system to achieve the business requirements

e Provide the Documentation for the settings and Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that allow
other consultants to do further changes or modifications if required.

e Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever required.

e Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the configured

processes in SAP.

e Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes in line with the
problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Support: ABAP

e Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

e Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields, selection
criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code change

e Maintenance of Customized Transactions

e Performance tuning

e SAP Note applications

e Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP

e Working closely with functional teams

e Support in analyzing & resolving the dump issues reported by the users

BASIS

e Review and Analyse Daily, Weekly and Annual System Monitoring Checks.

e Assignment of Roles and Authorisations to end users as per approval regularly without delay
to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorisations. (Like: CTS

move, User & Profile assignment and small correction in profile)

e Troubleshoot and rectify the problems on day-to-day basis

e Client copy / system refresh (whenever required at least once ina year)

e SAP note applications with reference to SAP Basis

e SNC configuration (whenever required at least once ina year).

e Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP Basis

e Performance Tuning on Periodic Basis

e Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests. The upload
data formats will be provided by the Consultants. NMDC will provide the cleansed and
validated data to the Support team.

ANNEXURE - B
Scope of Work for the proposal of Selection of Service Provider for Annual
Maintenance Contract for SAP S/4 HANA at NMDC
The scope of work in this tender includes
p> To provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted
day to day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP
System at all locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP
landscape implemented in NMDC Limited (NMDC) including developments to be made
as per the requirements of business notified from time to time.

a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)

b) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM) INCLUDING SUPPLIER RELATIONSHIP
MANAGEMENT (SRM)

c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)

d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)

e) HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT (HCM) / EMPLOYEE SELF SERVICE (ESS) /
MANAGEMENT SELF SERVICE (MSS)

f) PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)

g) SALES & DISTRIBUTION (SD)

h) QUALITY MANAGEMENT (QM)

i) ENVIRONMENT, HEALTH & SAFETY (EHS)

j) ADVANCED BUSINESS APPLICATION PROGRAMMING (ABAP)

k) BUSINESS APPLICATION SYSTEMS INTEGRATED SOLUTION (BASIS)

1) MANUFACTURING INTEGRATION INTELLIGENCE (MII)

m) FILE LIFECYCLE MANAGEMNET (FLM), FIORI APPS, MANAGEMENT
DASHBOARDS, NON-SAP PORTALS (PYTHON BASED PORTAL LIKE MDM, E-
MEASUREMENT BOOK ETC DEVELOPED IN-HOUSE)

In respect of NMDC, support shall be extended from Head Office, Hyderabad to all
locations. The major locations of NMDC are mentioned hereunder for information:

i. BIOM, Kirandul Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

ii, BIOM, Bacheli Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

iii. DIOM, Donimalai Complex, Bellary (Karnataka)
iv. Pellet Plant, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)
v. DMP, Panna. (Madhya Pradesh)
vi. SIU, Paloncha (Andhra Pradesh)
vii. Slurry Pipe Line (SPL), Jagdalpur, (Chhattisgarh)
viii. R&D Centre, Hyderabad (Telangana)
ix. Coal Division, Ranchi
x. Regional Offices Delhi, Raipur, Visakhapatnam, Chennai, Bengaluru, Mumbai,
Bhubaneshwar, Kolkata

However, wherever required, the support team has to travel to respective
locations as per the instructions of NMDC. The Conveyance & Lodging charges

SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support
Services including enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC
mentioned hereunder. It may note that the scope mentioned hereunder is not
exhaustive:
SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:
i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support
services which include the identification and rectification of all defects/bugs.

ii. The successful bidder is required to assist in providing solution for recovery
from transaction errors, system errors, hardware failures and/or other
application, modification, enhancement issues etc.

iii. The successful bidder is required to assist in performing period end closing
activities (Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting,
Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. The support team should be based at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad on full time
basis in respect of NMDC to facilitate timely resolution of issues.

v. Enhancements/developments, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as
applicable) are to be installed time to time where ever required on release by
OEM in consultation with the company including third party solutions
implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be accompanied by
a detailed document containing the step-by-step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance
(GRC) in the system to streamline and have better control across the transactions
of the company.

vii. The Successful Bidder shall assign the Roles and Authorizations to end users as
and when required as per approval without delay to ensure no business
transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorizations.

viii. The successful bidder shall provide contact numbers, email addresses, escalation
matrix of a dedicated support team. NMDC shall be kept informed well in advance
in case any changes are being made in the contact details.


---

Page 396 (pytesseract-ocr):
- 234

ix. The successful bidder shall submit detailed documentation (e.g. Technical
documents, Configuration documents, Functional document if any) for each
resolved issue GM ERP, NMDC.

x. The Management Dashboards has been developed and configured through Qlik
Sense Software in respect of NMDC. The Scope of support comprises
maintenance of existing Dashboards including addition / deletion / modification
of specific views as per the business requirements.

xi. For changes pertaining to completely new processes, or any new developments
including Dashboards etc., the total efforts (man days) required for the change
requests will be mutually agreed by both the successful bidder and Owner. If the
total efforts required are up to 5 man days, then the successful bidder has to
implement the changes free of cost. In case of changes requiring efforts more
than 5 man days, the successful bidder has to obtain approval of Owner with
financial implications before carrying out the changes. The financial implication
will be calculated by reducing the agreed effort by 5 man days and then
multiplying the balance man days with the rate (per man days) quoted in the
commercial/price bid by the successful bidder. It also includes integration with
third party software planned in future, if any.

xii. The successful bidder has to develop a tracking system Module Wise to record
the issues from “raising of ticket” by the user / ERP Module Core Member till
providing resolution.

xiii. Computers/laptops for office work of staff of successful bidder shall be in the
scope of successful bidder. All other services required to administer, maintain,
update and upgrade the SAP ERP System as per standard of performance
described in the Tender are included in the scope of Services of successful bidder.

xiv. The successful bidder will maintain the SAP ERP System in such manner so as to
minimize operating cost and maximize the system availability.

xv. The successful bidder has to prepare various documents including
Administration Manual, User Manuals, Standard Operating Procedure, Standard
Maintenance Procedure, etc for SAP ERP System and SAP Software.

xvi. The successful bidder shall submit all the necessary documentation (i.e.,
Functional Specifications, Technical Specifications, List of affected codes, Test
Scripts, Unit Test Plans, System Integration Test, User Acceptance Test Plans etc.)
for any changes they perform in the system. All these documents should be
approved by NMDC.

xvii. The successful bidder also has to provide all temporary work, ancillary work,
enabling work etc. including provision of tools & software required for
performing Services.

Monitoring and health check activities for smooth running of the servers.

xxi. The successful bidder shall prepare weekly/monthly status reports and submit
to the NMDC.

when required without any additional cost to NMDC.

phases of the project in writing to avoid any debatable issues of delaying the
project on accounts of reasons attributable to both the sides

successful bidder may share their comments on quality of tickets, any major

Role of SAP Functional Consultant

a. Assist business users for the problems/issues encountered during day-to-day
business transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

b. Study and evaluate the users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team
Members & User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an
abstract and algorithmic business model or System Requirement Specifications.

c. Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or
alternative solution is not available, solution may be provided through
configuration and customizing the respective business area and ensure to map
the system to achieve the business requirements

d. Provide the Documentation for the settings and Standard Operating
Procedures (SOP) that allow other consultants to do further changes or
modifications if required.

e. Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever
required.

f. Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the
configured scenarios in respect of new developments to be made.

g. Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with
gap analysis and get it approved through Core Team Members before starting
to configuration of future business processes in SAP.

h. Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes
in line with the problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Consultant: ABAP

a. Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

b. Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields,
selection criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code
change

c. Maintenance of Customized Transactions

} (3,....s\
i


---

Page 401 (pytesseract-ocr):
d. Performance tuning
e. SAP Note applications
f. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP
g. Working closely with functional teams
h. Support in analyzing & resolving the dump issues reported by the users
Role of SAP Technical Consultant: BASIS
a. Review and Analyze Daily, Weekly, Monthly and Annual System Monitoring
Checks.
b. Assignment of Roles and Authorizations to end users as per approval regularly
without delay to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles
& Authorizations. (Like: CTS move, User & Profile assignment and small
correction in profile)
c. Troubleshoot and rectify the problems on day to day basis
d. Client copy / system refresh (whenever required at least once in a year)
e. SAP note applications with reference to SAP Basis
f. SNC configuration (whenever required at least once in a year).
g. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP Basis
h. Performance Tuning on Periodic Basis and ensure 99.9% uptime
i. Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests.
The upload data formats will be provided by the Consultants. NMDC will provide
the cleansed and validated data to the Support team.

ANNEXURE - B
Scope of Work for the proposal of Selection of Service Provider for Annual
Maintenance Contract for SAP S/4 HANA at NMDC
The scope of work in this tender includes
p> To provide all the services (onsite support) required for successful and uninterrupted
day to day operations/transactions, maintenance, update and upgrade of the SAP ERP
System at all locations for the following modules of SAP S/4 HANA and entire SAP
landscape implemented in NMDC Limited (NMDC) including developments to be made
as per the requirements of business notified from time to time.

a) FINANCE & CONTROLLING (FICO)

b) MATERIALS MANAGEMENT (MM) INCLUDING SUPPLIER RELATIONSHIP
MANAGEMENT (SRM)

c) PRODUCTION PLANNING (PP)

d) PLANT MAINTENACE (PM)

e) HUMAN CAPITAL MANAGEMENT (HCM) / EMPLOYEE SELF SERVICE (ESS) /
MANAGEMENT SELF SERVICE (MSS)

f) PROJECT SYSTEM (PS)

g) SALES & DISTRIBUTION (SD)

h) QUALITY MANAGEMENT (QM)

i) ENVIRONMENT, HEALTH & SAFETY (EHS)

j) ADVANCED BUSINESS APPLICATION PROGRAMMING (ABAP)

k) BUSINESS APPLICATION SYSTEMS INTEGRATED SOLUTION (BASIS)

1) MANUFACTURING INTEGRATION INTELLIGENCE (MII)

m) FILE LIFECYCLE MANAGEMNET (FLM), FIORI APPS, MANAGEMENT
DASHBOARDS, NON-SAP PORTALS (PYTHON BASED PORTAL LIKE MDM, E-
MEASUREMENT BOOK ETC DEVELOPED IN-HOUSE)

In respect of NMDC, support shall be extended from Head Office, Hyderabad to all
locations. The major locations of NMDC are mentioned hereunder for information:

i. BIOM, Kirandul Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

ii, BIOM, Bacheli Complex, Dantewada. (Chhattisgarh)

iii. DIOM, Donimalai Complex, Bellary (Karnataka)
iv. Pellet Plant, Donimalai, Bellary (Karnataka)
v. DMP, Panna. (Madhya Pradesh)
vi. SIU, Paloncha (Andhra Pradesh)
vii. Slurry Pipe Line (SPL), Jagdalpur, (Chhattisgarh)
viii. R&D Centre, Hyderabad (Telangana)
ix. Coal Division, Ranchi
x. Regional Offices Delhi, Raipur, Visakhapatnam, Chennai, Bengaluru, Mumbai,
Bhubaneshwar, Kolkata

However, wherever required, the support team has to travel to respective
locations as per the instructions of NMDC. The Conveyance & Lodging charges
will be borne by owner as per the terms mentioned in this tender.
The successful bidder has to tie up with Existing Contractor for Knowledge
Transfer of the SAP ERP System, software developments, Source / _ software
codes, drawings, documents, manuals, reports, transactions & maintenance
records etc.
SCOPE OF WORK for SAP Support Service
The broad scope of the Functional and Technical consultant for SAP Support
Services including enhancement / customization of the SAP application in NMDC
mentioned hereunder. It may note that the scope mentioned hereunder is not
exhaustive:
SPECIFIC REQUIREMENT:
i. The successful bidder is required to provide technical & functional support
services which include the identification and rectification of all defects/bugs.

ii. The successful bidder is required to assist in providing solution for recovery
from transaction errors, system errors, hardware failures and/or other
application, modification, enhancement issues etc.

iii. The successful bidder is required to assist in performing period end closing
activities (Monthly, Quarterly, and Annually) including Tax reporting,
Regulatory Compliance etc.

iv. The support team should be based at NMDC Head Office, Hyderabad on full time
basis in respect of NMDC to facilitate timely resolution of issues.

v. Enhancements/developments, patches, version upgrades, service packs etc. (as
applicable) are to be installed time to time where ever required on release by
OEM in consultation with the company including third party solutions
implemented and integrated with SAP. Each upgrade should be accompanied by
a detailed document containing the step-by-step procedure for effecting the
changes to the system by ensuing an established change management process.

vi. The successful bidder shall review and adopt the Governance, Risk & Compliance
(GRC) in the system to streamline and have better control across the transactions
of the company.

vii. The Successful Bidder shall assign the Roles and Authorizations to end users as
and when required as per approval without delay to ensure no business
transactions was halted for want of Roles & Authorizations.

viii. The successful bidder shall provide contact numbers, email addresses, escalation
matrix of a dedicated support team. NMDC shall be kept informed well in advance
in case any changes are being made in the contact details.


---

Page 411 (pytesseract-ocr):
219

ix. cessful bidder shall submit detailed documentation (e.g. Technical
do nts, Configuration documents, Functional document if any) for each
resolved issue GM ERP, NMDC.

x. The Management Dashboards has been developed and configured through Qlik
Sense Software in respect of NMDC. The Scope of support comprises
maintenance of existing Dashboards including addition / deletion / modification
of specific views as per the business requirements.

xi. For changes pertaining to completely new processes, or any new developments
including Dashboards etc., the total efforts (man days) required for the change
requests will be mutually agreed by both the successful bidder and Owner. If the
total efforts required are up to 5 man days, then the successful bidder has to
implement the changes free of cost. In case of changes requiring efforts more
than 5 man days, the successful bidder has to obtain approval of Owner with
financial implications before carrying out the changes. The financial implication
will be calculated by reducing the agreed effort by 5 man days and then
multiplying the balance man days with the rate (per man days) quoted in the
commercial/price bid by the successful bidder. It also includes integration with
third party software planned in future, if any.

xii. The successful bidder has to develop a tracking system Module Wise to record
the issues from “raising of ticket” by the user / ERP Module Core Member till
providing resolution.

xiii. Computers/laptops for office work of staff of successful bidder shall be in the
scope of successful bidder. All other services required to administer, maintain,
update and upgrade the SAP ERP System as per standard of performance
described in the Tender are included in the scope of Services of successful bidder.

xiv. The successful bidder will maintain the SAP ERP System in such manner so as to
minimize operating cost and maximize the system availability.

xv. The successful bidder has to prepare various documents including
Administration Manual, User Manuals, Standard Operating Procedure, Standard
Maintenance Procedure, etc for SAP ERP System and SAP Software.

xvi. The successful bidder shall submit all the necessary documentation (i.e.,
Functional Specifications, Technical Specifications, List of affected codes, Test
Scripts, Unit Test Plans, System Integration Test, User Acceptance Test Plans etc.)
for any changes they perform in the system. All these documents should be
approved by NMDC.

xvii. The successful bidder also has to provide all temporary work, ancillary work,
enabling work etc. including provision of tools & software required for
performing Services.

xviii. The successful bidder shall provide adequate training as well as awareness
programs to all users in respective modules or as and when requested by the
Owner without impacting the day to day transactions.

xix. The Basis Consultants of the successful bidder will perform all type of monitoring
and administrative activities like restarting SAP servers, maintenance of backup
data, maintenance of system configuration, administration for Browser-Based
User Dialogs, User Administration (ABAP/JAVA/PYTHON etc.), Creating and
maintaining Remote Function Call (RFC) Connections, Administration and
Maintenance for External Communication, Schedule Background Tasks, Printer
and spool administration / maintenance, Administration and maintenance of
Transport Management System, System Monitoring and Trouble Shooting,
Setting Up an SAP System Landscape, SLD monitoring, Exporting/importing
Transport Requests, , System Workload Analysis, System trace monitoring,
Performance Analysis, SAP Memory Management , System Load Verification,
Expensive SQL Statements Analysis, SAP Table buffering, Database Overview and
monitoring, DB Monitoring Tools, Recovery, DBA activity through BR*Tools,
Restoring a backup without any additional cost to the Owner.

xx. The successful bidder will perform SAP BASIS activities in Development, Quality
and production SAP S/4 HANA systems. They will also perform all the System
Monitoring and health check activities for smooth running of the servers.

xxi. The successful bidder shall prepare weekly/monthly status reports and submit
to the NMDC.

xxii. The successful bidder shall take up any SAP product level issues with SAP
through respective module lead of NMDC and shall also follow up on the same
for quick resolution.

xxiii. If any support is required pertaining to SAP applications in DC & DR sites then
same shall be extended by the successful bidder to the DATA CENTER (DC) &
DISASTER RECOVERY (DR) site without any additional cost.

xxiv. The successful bidder will follow the holiday calendar of NMDC. They have to
work late after working hours and attend issues on weekends/holidays as and
when required without any additional cost to NMDC.

xxv. Successful bidder shall keep each & every record, logs of the proceedings of each
phases of the project in writing to avoid any debatable issues of delaying the
project on accounts of reasons attributable to both the sides

xxvi. Successful bidder shall submit monthly detailed report to GM ERP NMDC on
Helpdesk ticket analysis with all status, Change Request status and status of all
service requests logged with OEM SAP and also make suitable presentation to
the management on the above issues as and when required. Additionally,
successful bidder may share their comments on quality of tickets, any major

Minimum qualifications requirement for resources:
Project Manager | BE / B. TECH / MBA / MCA / CA/ ICWA degree with minimum 10

Page 415 (pytesseract-ocr):
Role of SAP Functional Consultant

a. Assist business users for the problems/issues encountered during day-to-day
business transactions for smooth functioning of the ERP System.

b. Study and evaluate the users’ requirements by interacting with the Core Team
Members & User Departments of NMDC and transform the essence into an
abstract and algorithmic business model or System Requirement Specifications.

c. Realize the solution through standard features of SAP, if standard feature or
alternative solution is not available, solution may be provided through
configuration and customizing the respective business area and ensure to map
the system to achieve the business requirements

d. Provide the Documentation for the settings and Standard Operating
Procedures (SOP) that allow other consultants to do further changes or
modifications if required.

e. Providing proper training to the Users and Core Team Members where ever
required.

f. Functional consultants are also expected to prepare test scripts for testing the
configured scenarios in respect of new developments to be made.

g. Preparation of the documents like As-is-study, Business Blue Print along with
gap analysis and get it approved through Core Team Members before starting
to configuration of future business processes in SAP.

h. Performing minor Configuration changes to resolve existing business processes
in line with the problem on hand.

Role of SAP Technical Consultant: ABAP

a. Bug Fixing (Program errors, Incorrect data display, etc.)

b. Modifications to any custom objects to accommodate addition of new fields,
selection criterion, data display as long as this doesn’t result into a major code
change

c. Maintenance of Customized Transactions

} (3,....s\
i


---

Page 416 (pytesseract-ocr):
d. Performance tuning
e. SAP Note applications
f. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP
g. Working closely with functional teams
h. Support in analyzing & resolving the dump issues reported by the users
Role of SAP Technical Consultant: BASIS
a. Review and Analyze Daily, Weekly, Monthly and Annual System Monitoring
Checks.
b. Assignment of Roles and Authorizations to end users as per approval regularly
without delay to ensure no business transactions was halted for want of Roles
& Authorizations. (Like: CTS move, User & Profile assignment and small
correction in profile)
c. Troubleshoot and rectify the problems on day to day basis
d. Client copy / system refresh (whenever required at least once in a year)
e. SAP note applications with reference to SAP Basis
f. SNC configuration (whenever required at least once in a year).
g. Handling of Application Integration issues that fall in the purview of SAP Basis
h. Performance Tuning on Periodic Basis and ensure 99.9% uptime
i. Support will be extended for Data Migrations for the approved change requests.
The upload data formats will be provided by the Consultants. NMDC will provide
the cleansed and validated data to the Support team.
Signature Not Verified

ANNEXURE - 3 INTEGRITY PACT
Between
NMDC Limited, hereinafter referred te as “The Principal"/ Employer
And
Ernst & Young LLP hereinafter referred to as “The Bidder / Contractor”
Preamble

The Principal intends to award, under laid down organizational procedures, contract/s for

(Description of the Equipment). The Principal values full compliance with all relevant laws and

regulations, and the principles of economical use of resources, and of fairmess and transparency in its

relation with its Bidder/s and for Contractor/'s.

In order to achieve these goals, the Principal Cooperates with the renowned international Non-

Governmental Organization “Transparency International” (Tl. Following TI's national and

international experience, the Principal will appoint an external independent Monitor who will

monitor the tender process from the beginning till execution of the contract for compliance with
the principles mentioned HEREIN.
Section 1- Commitments of the Principal.

(2) The Principal commits itself to take all measures necessary to prevent corruption and to observe
the following principles:--

Il No employee of the Principal, personally or through family members, will in connection with
the tender for, or the execution of a contract demand, take a promise for or accept, for
him/herself or third person, any material benefit which he/she is not legally entitled to.

I. «The Principal will, during the tender process treat all Bidders with equity and reason. The
principal willin particular, before and during the tender process, provide to all Bidders the same
information and will not provide to any Bidder confidential/additional information through
which the Bidder could obtain an advantage in relation to the tender process or the contract
execution.

OL The Principal will exclude from the process all known prejudiced persons.

(2) | if the Principal obtains information on the conduct of any of its employees which is a criminal
offence under the relevant Anti-Corruption Laws of India, or if there be a substantive suspicion in
this regard, the Principal will inform its Vigilance Office and in addition can initiate disciplinary
action.

SECTION - 2 Commitments of the Bidder/Contractor

(1) The Bidder / Contractor commit himself to take all measures necessary to prevent corruption.
He commits himself to observe the following principles during his participation in the tender
process and during the contract execution.

I. The Bidder / Contractor will not, directly or through any other person or firm, offer, promise or
give to the Principal, to any of the Principal's employee involved in the tender process or the
execution of the contract or to any third person any material or immaterial benefit which he/she
is not legally entitled to, in order to obtain in exchange an advantage during the tender process
or the execution of the contract.

I. The Bidder / Contractor will net enter with other Bidders into any illegal agreement or
understanding, whether formal or informal. This applies in particular to prices, specifications,
certifications, subsidiary contracts, submission or non-submission of bids or action to restrict
competitiveness.

Il. The Bidder / Contractor will not commit any criminal offence under the relevant Anti-corruption
Laws of India, further the Bidder/Contractor will not use improperly, for purposes of competition or
personal gain, or pass on to cthers, any information provided by the Principal as part of the business
relationship, regarding plans, technical proposals and business details, including information
contained or transmitted electronically.

TV. The Bidder/Contractor will, when presenting his bid, disclose any and all payments he has made,
is committed to or intends to make to agents, brokers or any other intermediaries in connection
with the award of the contract.

(2) The Bidder / Contractor will not instigate third persons to commit offences outlined above or be
an accessory to such offences.

(3) The Bidder / Contractor may indicate the advantage of his offer compared to the tender terms
and conditions. The Bidder / Contractor shall not make any commitment whatsoever on the
offers / products of other bidder(s) thereby influencing the principal to take decision of the
former.

(4) A person signing Integrity Pact shall not approach the Courts while representing the matters to
IEMs and he/she will wait their decision in the matter.

(5) Foreign bidders to disclose the name and address of agents and representatives in India and
Indian Bidders to disclose their foreign principals or associate.

(6) Bidders to disclose the payments to be made them to agents/brokers or any other intermediary.

(7) Bidders to disclose any transgressions with any other company that may impinge on the anti-
corruption principle.

Section 3 - Disqualification from tender process and exclusion from future contract

1. lf the Bidder, before contract award, has committed a serious transgression through a violation
of Section 2 or in any other form such as to put his reliability or credibility as Bidder into
question, the Principal is entitled to disqualify the Bidder from the tender process or to
terminate the contract, if already signed, for such reason.

2. lithe Bidder/Contractor has committed a serious transgression through a violation of section - 2
such as to put his reliability or credibility into question, the principal is entitled also to exclude the
Bidder / Contractor from future contract award processes. The imposition and duration of the
exclusion will be determined by the severity of the transgression. The severity will be determined
by the circumstances of the case, in particular the number of transgressions, the position of the
transgressors with the company hierarchy of the Bidder and the amount of the damage. The
exclusion will be imposed for a minimum of 6 months and maximum of 3 years.

3. ifthe Bidder / Contractor can prove that he has restored/recouped the damage caused by himand
has installed a suitable corruption prevention system, the Principal may revoke the exclusion
prematurely.

4. A transgression is considered to have occurred if in light of available evidence no reasonable
doubt is possible.

Section 4 - Forfeiture of Earnest Money Deposit/Security Deposit

(1) If the Principal has disqualified the Bidder from the tender process prior to the award according
to Section 3, the Principal is entitled to forfeit the bidders Earnest Money Deposit.

(2) If the Principal has terminated the contract according to section - 3, or if the Principal is entitled
to terminate the contract according to section - 3, the principal shall be entitled to forfeit the
Earnest Money Deposit/Security Deposit.

Section 5 - Previous Transgression

(1) The Bidder declares that mo previous transgression occurred in the last three years with any
other company in any country confirming to the Tl approach or with any other Public Sector
Enterprise in India that could justify its exclusion from the tender process.

(2) If the bidder makes incorrect statement on this subject, he can be disqualified from the tender
process or the contract, if already awarded, can be terminated for such reason.

Section 6 - Equal treatment of all bidders/contractors/sub-contractors.

(1) The bidder/contractor undertakes to demand from all sub-contractors the commitment
consistent with this integrity pact, and to submit it to the Principal before contract signing.

(2) The principal will enter into agreement with identical conditions as this one with all
bidders, contractors and sub-contractors.

(3) The principal will disqualify from the tender process all bidders who do not sign this pact and
submit it to the Principal along with the offer.

Section 7 - Criminal charges violating Bidders/Contractors/Sub-Contractors

If the principal obtains knowledge of conduct of a Bidders/Contractors/Sub-Contractors, or of an

employee or a representative or an associate of a Bidders/Contractors/Sub-Contractors which

constitutes corruption, or if the principal has substantive suspicion in this regard, the principal will
inform the vigilance office.
Section & - External Independent Monitor

(1) The principal appoints competent and credible external independent Monitor for this Pact. The
task of the monitor is to review independently and objectively, whether and to what extent the
parties comply with the obligations under this agreement.

(2) The monitor is not subject to instructions by the representatives of the parties and performs
his functions neutrally and independently. He reports to the Chairman of the Board of the
Principal.

(3) The Monitor has the right of access without restriction to all Projects documentation of the
Principal. The Contractor will also grant the monitor, upon his request and demonstration of a
valid interest, unlimited access to his project documentation. The same is applicable to
Subcontractors. The Monitor is under contractual obligation to treat the information and
documents of the Bidder/Contractor/Subcontractor with confidentiality.

(4) The Principal will provide to the Monitor sufficient information about all meetings among the
parties related to the Project provided such meetings could have an impact on the contractual
relations between the Principal and the Contractor. The parties offer to the Monitor the option to
participate in such meetings.

¢S) As soon as the Monitor notices, or believes to notice, a violation of this agreement, he will so inform
the Management of the Principal and request the Management to discontinue or heal the violation,
or take other relevant action. The monitor can in this regard submit non-binding
recommendations. Beyond this, the Monitor has no right to demand from the parties that they act
in 4 specific manner, refrain from action or tolerate action.

(6) The Monitor will regularly submit a written report to the Chairman of the Board of the Principal
and, should the occasion arise, submit proposals for correcting problematic situations.

CF) lf the Monitor has reported to the Chairman of the Board a substantiated suspicion of an offence
under relevant Anti-Corruption Laws of India, and the Chairman has not, with reasonable time,
taken visible action to proceed against such offence or reported it to the Vigilance Officer, the
Monitor may also transmit this information directly to the Central Vigilance Commissioner,
Government of India.

Section 9 - Pact Duration
This Pact begins when both parties have legally signed it. It expires forthe Contractor 12 months after
the last payment under the respective contract, and for all other Bidder's 6 months after the contract
has been awarded.
Section 10 - Other Provisions

LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT
ERNST & YOUNG LLP

THIS LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT (the "LLP Agreement’) Is made on 4th day of

March 2013 by and among:

Persons whose names and residential addresses are set out in Appendix-A hereto, hereinafter

referred to collectively as “Parties” or “Partners” and individually as “Party” or “Partner")

WHEREAS:

A ERNST & YOUNG PRIVATE LIMITED C"EYPL") having Company Identification Number
U74120WB2006PTC121768 has been engaged in the business of providing services in the
areas of taxhtion, risk and business advisory and transaction advisory under the name and
style of ‘ERNST & YOUNG PRIVATE LIMITED’;

8. The Partners are the shareholders in EYPL;

c. The Partners desire to convert EYPL into a limited liability partnership and carry on the
business of providing services in the areas of taxation, risk and business advisory and
transaction advisory in all its spheres that are carried on by EYPL, in the form of a limited
lability partnership; and

a The Partners have agreed to form a limited liability partnership pursuant to the Limited
Liability Partnership Act 2008, in the name and style of ‘ERNST & YOUNG LLP’ ("LLP™), on
the terms and subject to the conditions set forth hereinafter.

THE PARTIES HERETO AGREE AS FOLLOWS:

1 DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION

1.1 Definitions
In this Agreement, the following expressions shall have, where the context admits, the
meaning assigned to those expressions herein below:

“Accounts” means the statutory accounts of the LLP prepared in accordance with generally

accepted accounting principles in india;

“Accounting / Fiscal Year” means the accounting year of the LLP, which commences on

April 1 every year and ends on March 31 of the succeeding year, or such other date as may

be approved by the Executive Board; Provided however, the first accounting year / period of

the LLP shall commence on the Effective Date and end on 31° March 2014;

“Affiliated Entity” means and inchudes all entities, present and future, in India, their

successors and assigns who are member firms of Ernst & Young Global Limited

“Allocable Profits” has the meaning given in Article 17.2.2; WW |

“Applicable Laws” means all laws, rules reguiation, bye laws, regulations and ordinances etc.
of India including but not limited to the Companies Act 1956 and rules made thereunder, The
Limited Liability Partnership Act 2008 and rules made thereunder, including all amendments
made thereto from time to time;
“Business Day" means a day on which commercial banks are open for ordinary business in
India;
“Committee” means a committee established pursuant to Article 30;
“Competing Network" means any Entity or network of Entities (other than members of a
club/organisation/Entity of which the LLP is a member) which Is, or is likely to be, engaged in
activities identical or similer to the activities of the LLP or such member of the
club/Entity/organisation referred to herein.
“Confidential Information” has the meaning given in Article 33;
“Departing Partner” means a Partner whose membership interest in the LLP has Terminated
or in relation to whom a Termination Notice has been served;
“Dispute” has the meaning given in Article 36;
“Effective Date” means the date of registration as specified on the Certificate of Registration
of Conversion as Issued by the Registrar;
“Entity” means a company, limited liability partnership (other than the LLP formed by this
agreement), partnership, trust or society or other entity constituted and also includes, where
the context so permits, a natural person;
“Executive Board" has the meaning given to it in Article 24;
“Former Partner” means a Person who has been a Partner, but Is no longer such a Partner
in the LLP;
“Garden Leave Period" means the period during which a Partner Is on garden leave pursuant
to Article 10;
“Governmental Authority” means any supra-national, national, federal, state, regional,
municipal or local government, authority, agency, assembly or other body, court, central
bank or regulatory or taxing body;
“Intellectual Property” means patents, trademarks, service marks, trade names, domain
names, logos, get-up, Inventions, registered and unregistered design rights, copyrights,
database rights and all other similar rights in any part of the world Cincluding know-how)
including, where such rights are obtained or enhanced by registration, any registration of
such rights and applications and rights to apply for such registrations;

criteria, if the required parameters as stated above are fulfilled.
e.g., if a bidder has 05 years of Annual Maintenance Contract from a customer/client and it has
successfully completed 01 year of Annual Maintenance then the completed period shall be considered
for evaluation, if relevant documents as mentioned above are provided by the bidder.

order / contract agreement and completion certificate of previous works executed by them mentioning
name, address and contact telephone numbers of issuing authority as per Annexure-5 of ITT.

accompanied with TDS certificate issued by the client / 26 AS for that work.

only related to shortfall information of the submitted documents at the time of tender opening.

for evaluation of eligibility criteria.

| SAP R/3 System: Production Server Details ets

CSL. is currently using SAP ERP System to carry aut its day to day business processes. CSi. seeks
to migrate the existing SAP ECC system to the latest SAP S/4HANA System for paving a way for
enhanced quality. improved speed of operations and intelligent automations. CSL doesn’t intend
to redefine or re-engineer its existing business processes but seeks to do the technical upgrade of
the existing SAP ECC System by migrating/converting it into the latest S/4IIAMLA platform
{Brownfield Implementation),
‘The entire project involves the five major activities as furnished below;
1, SAP SYSTEMS UPGRADE & S/4HANA CONVERSION WITH POST GO-LIVE SUPPORT [{
PGLS FOR 3 MONTHS STABILISATION PERIOD)
2, SUPPORT SERVICES DURING THE WARRANTY PERIOD

Annexure A: Statement of work
This Statement of Work, dated Jan 5, 2018 <this “SOW"), is made by Ernst and Young
LLP (“we" or “EY”) and Smartchem Technologies Ltd. ("STL", “you” or “Client”,
pursuant to the Agreement, dated Jan 5, 2018 (the "“Agreement”), between EY and
STL.
Except as otherwise set forth in this SOW, this SOW incorporates by reference, and is
deemed to be a part of, the Agreement. The additional terrns and conditions of this
SOW shall apply only to the Services covered by this SOW and not to Services covered
by any other Statement of Work pursuant to the Agreement. Capitalized terms used,
but not otherwise defined, in this SOW shall have the meanings in the Agreement, and
references in the Agreement to “you” or “Client” shall be deemed references to you.
Background
Smartchem Technologies Limited (STL) is a leading manufacturer of fertilizers and
Technical Ammonium Nitrate (TAN) in western India. The Client is based in Pune and
has manufacturing operations in Taloja (MH) and Srikakulam (AP).
The Client has a multi-product portfolio spanning crop nutrition business (CNB) of bulk
and specialty fertilizers, technical arnmonium nilrale (TAN), and associated
diagnostics and solutions. These diverse businesses are managed through two
business units viz.
* Crop Nutrition Business (CNB)
® Technical Ammonium Nitrate (TAN)
The Client has recently expanded manufacturing capacity in its CNB segment. It is
largeting production growth from approx 2.5 lakh TPA (FY17) lo approx 11 lakh TPA
(FY20). Going forward, the Client is aiming for similar growth across other business
segments as well, drawing strengths from its existing product and plant synergies and
adding new product lines, new / augmented manufacturing capacities and markets.
High growth, internal restructuring and market dynamics have brought a new set of
challenges for STL acrass mulliple areas:

1. Lack of a robust planning process results in low forecast accuracy leading lo
potential stock-outs and consequently low fill rates and loss of sales, specifically
across CNB & TAN - Domestic

2. Reactive planning for traded Ferlilizers in CNB leads to potential loss of sales
or slow moving inventory

3. Lack of focus on margin maximization leads to opportunity loss within existing
capacities & production... _

6. Limited storage availability prevents pre-build during lean season for TAN to

The planned growth reauires focus on following areas that need to be strengthened and
re-designed:
Supply Chain strategy & Inventory strategy which are segment specitic
Demand planning (statistical forecast and collaboration)
RCCP & Production Planning Qwith ability to optimize high-margin products)
Allocation strategy

Cause Analysis

differen areas, STL has decided to undertake an Integrated Transformation journey

» Sales and Distribulion Effectiveness
s Sales and Operations Planning
® Process and Technology Effectiveness (SAP+BPR+DoA) enabled on SAP S4H

segments:

Objective of Study
The objeclives of fhe proposed engagement under each of the modules are outlined
below
Sales and Distribution Effectiveness
EY will partner with STL to plan/ design and drive sales and distribution transformation
for its Crop Nutrition business (CNB). The implementation would be focused on a set
of LO8 districts covering & States (Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana,
Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Uttar Pradesh). These districts
contribute to 91% of turnover currently.
As part of the S&D transformation module, EY would focus on improving distribution
effectiveness, sales force effectiveness, sales & marketing spend optimization, and
designing and facilitating execution of a Farmer Connect program.
Sales and Operations Planning
The objectives of the module are to deliver benefits for STL spanning Revenue, Cost &
Capital levers:
1, Revenue Enhancement / Lost Sales Reduction
a. Improved Fill Rates from better FG Stock Norms and placement

b. Improved Service Level adherence leading to lesser loss of potential

2. Volume Maximization
a. Improve RM availability through better planning to minimize production

3. Cost & Capital Optimization

The engagement also envisages other intangible and overall efficiency ralated benefits
covering:
1. Standardized operations and processes
2. Clear cross-functional SLAs and performance management leading to lesser time
spent on fire fighting
3. Improved planning and decision making process ensuring timely and efficient
execution of operational as well as tactical activities
4. Improved visibility and responsiveness in the supply chain
5. Structured planning and adherence / tracking Lo budget
The key objectives of this module are
1. Process simplification through automation and elimination of redundancies
2. Strengthen linkage between function, processes and systems
3. Designing scalable and replicable processes
4, Clearly define responsibilities and accountabilities
5. Enhance effectiveness of decision making by identifying key decision areas &
extent of delegation, aligning organizational structure, competency and risk
appetite, and having adequate checks & balances without diluting
accountability and/or creating delays

foad space. HSPL has shawn growth in line with the industry over tne last few years. During this phase

i aforesaid Statement of Account give a tue and fair view in conformity with the accounting principles
| generally accepted in India, of the state of affairs of the Firm as at March 31, 2022, the profit and its cash
| flow for the year ended on that date.
! Basis for Opinion.
i We conducted our audit in accordance with the Standards on Auditing (SAs) issued by the Institute of
: ‘Chartered Accountants of India. Our responsibilities under those Standards are further described in the

; | independent of the Firm in accordance with the Code of Ethics issued by the Institute of Chartered
J Accountants of India together with the cthical requirements that are relevant to our audit of the Statement of

j the Code of Ethics. We believe that the audit evidence we have obtaincd is sufficient and appropriate to
ptovide a basis for our opinion.
Responsibilitics of Management for the Statement of Account :
The Firm’s Management is responsible for the preparation of these Statement of Account that give a true
and fair view of the financial position, financial performance, and cash flow of the Firm in accordance with
the accounting principles generally acccpted in India, including the accounting Standards issued by the
Chartered Accountants of India. This responsibility also includes selection and application of appropriate
accounting policies; making judgments and estimates that are reasonable and prudent; and, design,
implementation and maintenance of adequate internal controls relevant to the preparation and preseptation
of the Statement of Account that give a true and fair view and are free from material misstatement, whether
due to fraud or error. ’

i continue as a going concern, disclosing, as applicable, matters related to going concern and-using the’ going
i concern basis of accounting unless management either intends to dissolve the Firm or to cease operations, or

Auditor’s Responsibilities for the Audit of the Statement of Account
: Our objectives are to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the Statement of Account as a whole are
: free from material misstatement, whether due to fraud or error, and Lo issue an auditor’s report that includes fo.
our opinion. Reasonable assurance is a high level of assurance, but is not a guarantee that an audit conducted
in accordance with SAs will always detect a material misstatement when it exists. Misstatements can arise -
, from fraud ot error and are considered material if, individually or in the aggregate, they could reasonably be
7 expected to influence the cconomic decisions of users taken on the basis of these Statement of Account,

As part of an audit in accordance with SAs, we exercise professional judgment and maintain pto fessional

skepticism thtoughout the audit. We also:

+ Identify and assess the risks of material misstatement of the Statement of Account, whether due to fraud
or error, design and perform audit procedures responsive to those risks, and obtain audit evidence that is
sufficient and appropriate to provide a basis for our opinion. The risk of not detecting a material
misstatement resulting from fraud is higher than for one resulting from error, as fraud may involve
collusion, forgery, intentional omissions, misrepresentations, or the override of internal control.

, * Obtain an understanding of internal control relevant to the audit in order to design audit procedures that
are appropriate in the circumstances, but not for the purpose of expressing an opinion on the operating
: effectiveness of the entity’s internal controls.

+ Evaluate the appropriateness of accounting policies used and the reasonableness of accounting estimates

and related disclosures made by management.

* Conclude on the appropriateness of management’s use of the going concern basis of accounting and,

| based on the audit evidence obtained, whether a material uncertainty exists related to events or
Fad conditions that may cast significant doubt on the Firm’s ability to continue as a going concern. If we
, conclude that a material uncertainty exists, we ate required to draw attention in our auditor’s report to
the related disclosures in the Statement of Account or, if such disclosures ate inadequate, to modify our
: opinion. Our conclusions are based on the audit evidence obtained up to the date of our auditor’s report.
However, future events or conditions may causc the Firm to cease to continue as a going concern.
j ‘ * Evaluate the overall presentation, structure and content of the Statement of Account, including the
q ' . disclosures, and whether the Statement of Account represent the underlying transactions and events in a
H manner that achieves fair presentation. .

INDEPENDENT AUDITOR’S REPORT
To the Partners of Ermst & Young LLP .
Report on the Audit of the Statement of Account
Opinion
We have audited the accompanying Statement of Account of Ernst & Young LLP (‘the Firm’), which
comprise the Statement of Assets and Liabilities as at March 31, 2023, the Statement of Income and
Lxpenditure, the Statement of Cash Flow for the year then ended, and notes to the Statement of Account,
including a summary of significant accounting policies and other explanatory information.
In our opinion and to the best of our information and according to the explanations given to us, the
aforesaid Statement of Account give a truc and fair view in conformity with the accounting principles
generally accepted in India, of the state of affairs of the Firm as at March 31, 2023, the profit and its cash
flow for the year ended on that date.
Basis for Opinion
We conducted our audit in accordance with the Standards on Auditing (SAs) issued by the Institute of
Chartered Accountants of India. Our responsibilities under those Standards are further described in the
Auditor’s Responsibilities for the Audit of the Statement of Account section of our report. We are
independent of the Firm in accordance with the Code of Ethics issued by the Institute of Chartered
Accountants of India together with the ethical requirements that are relevant to our audit of the Statement of
Account and we have fulfilled our other ethical responsibilities in accordance with these requirements and
the Code of Ethics. We believe that the audit evidence we have obtained is sufficient and appropriate to
provide a basis for our opinion.
Responsibilities of Management for the Statement of Account
The Firm’s Management is responsible for the preparation of these Statement of Account that give a true
and fair view of the financial position, financial performance, and cash flow of the Firm in accordance with
the accounting principles generally accepted in India, including the accounting Standards issued by the
Chartered Accountants of India. This responsibility also includes selection and application of appropriate
accounting policies; making judgments and estimates that are reasonable and prudent; and design,
implementation and maintenance of adequate internal controls rclevant to the preparation and presentation
of the Statement of Account that give a true and fair view and are free from material misstatement, whether
due to fraud or error. .
In preparing the Statement of Account, management is responsible for assessing the Firm’s ability to .
continue as a going concern, disclosing, as applicable, matters related to going concern and using the going
concern basis of accounting unless management either intends to dissolve the Firm or to cease operations, or
has no realistic alternative but to do so.
